chunks.text: Japanese people are among the longest - lived in the world.
The average lifespan is eighty - seven for women and eighty - one for men
at the world's top level.
What is the secret?
Many researchers have tried to uncover this mystery.
The first factor is diet.
Traditional Japanese food includes vegetables, fish, fermented food, rice, and miso.
These are balanced in nutrition and do not contain excessive fat.
The second factor is lifestyle habits.
Japanese walk every day.
Even in cities, they commute by public transportation and walk to the station.
The elderly also keep walking as a habit.
The third factor is social connection.
As in Okinawa's " yuimaaru," local community is strong, and people are not isolated.
Okinawa especially is a well - known land of longevity
with many people over a hundred.
The fourth factor is " ikigai " — a reason for living.
Japanese people have the concept of ikigai.
To hold small goals and daily enjoyments.
After retirement, they occupy themselves with a vegetable garden or devote themselves to calligraphy.
But long life has its shadows too.
Dementia, nursing care, and the burden on social security.
The challenge is not only to live long, but to simultaneously extend " healthy lifespan.
Young people today live differently from their parents' generation.
Fast food, traveling by car after parking, a mixture of food cultures.
Will the next generation be able to maintain the same longevity?
Traditional food, the habit of walking, connection with community.
These are not old things, but a resource for the future.
To incorporate old wisdom into convenient modern life is the key to holding a healthy
long life across generations.
The secret of longevity is not a special medicine.
It is the accumulation of small daily choices.
